excerpt: Safety Nicaragua has a high crime rate, including armed robbery, assault and express kidnapping. Borders and remote areas have higher risks of violent crime due to organised crime activity.

Citizens.” More information Worldwide Caution: The Department of State advises Americans worldwide to exercise increased caution. government employees working in Nicaragua are generally not allowed to drive after dark due to safety risks. government employees working in Nicaragua are not allowed to use public transportation due to safety risks. government employees working in Nicaragua are not allowed to go to these places due to safety risks: Managua's Mercado Oriental Managua’s national baseball stadium Casinos and strip clubs Non-personal service contractors traveling to Nicaragua on official .. nationals are at serious risk of wrongful detention by the Government of Nicaragua.

Accept additional cookies Reject additional cookies View cookies Hide cookie message Skip to main content Foreign travel advice Nicaragua Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security Terrorism There is a high threat of terrorist attack globally affecting UK interests and British nationals, including from groups and individuals who view the UK and British nationals as targets. Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Political situation Demonstrations and gatherings can turn violent. You can be arrested and detained or deported for: taking part in a protest breach of the peace any activity seen as acting against the government, including political meetings and campaigns using Nicaragua’s national flag or colours (blue and white) for campaigning or protesting Demonstrations can also lead to travel disruption. Crime Violent crime Foreign nationals have been affected by violent crime in Nicaragua.
